div.h2: 3 Tables: A(100 rows), B(1000 rows), C(500 rows)

div.p: DP exploits optimal substructure: the best plan for {A,B,C} reuses the already-computed best plan for {A,C}. Complexity: O(3ⁿ) for n tables — tractable up to ~12 tables. Beyond that, switch to greedy or genetic algorithms.
Cost = 3 Est. rows = 100
Cost = 30 Est. rows = 1000
Cost = 15 Est. rows = 500
Option 1: A outer, B inner cost = dp[A] + dp[B] + A.rows × B.pages cost = 3 + 30 + 100 × 30 cost = 3 + 30 + 3000 = 3033
Option 2: B outer, A inner cost = dp[B] + dp[A] + B.rows × A.pages cost = 30 + 3 + 1000 × 3 cost = 30 + 3 + 3000 = 3033
✅ Winner: A outer (tie → smaller table) Cost = 3033, Est. rows = 1000
Join: A.x = C.z (if edge exists) NDV_A = 100, NDV_C = 100 Card = 100×500 / max(100,100) = 500
Option 1: A outer, C inner cost = dp[A] + dp[C] + A.rows × C.pages cost = 3 + 15 + 100 × 15 cost = 3 + 15 + 1500 = 1518
Option 2: C outer, A inner cost = dp[C] + dp[A] + C.rows × A.pages cost = 15 + 3 + 500 × 3 cost = 15 + 3 + 1500 = 1518
✅ Winner: A outer (tie → smaller table) Cost = 1518, Est. rows = 500
Option 1: B outer, C inner cost = dp[B] + dp[C] + B.rows × C.pages cost = 30 + 15 + 1000 × 15 cost = 30 + 15 + 15000 = 15045
Option 2: C outer, B inner cost = dp[C] + dp[B] + C.rows × B.pages cost = 15 + 30 + 500 × 30 cost = 15 + 30 + 15000 = 15045
✅ Winner: C outer (tie → smaller table) Cost = 15045, Est. rows = 10000
cost = dp[{A}] + dp[{B,C}] + A.rows × inner_probe cost = 3 + 15045 + 100 × 15 (C inner of BC) = 16548 Est. rows = card(A ⋈ BC)
cost = dp[{B}] + dp[{A,C}] + B.rows × inner_probe cost = 30 + 1518 + 1000 × 3 (A inner of AC) = 4548 Est. rows = card(B ⋈ AC)
cost = dp[{C}] + dp[{A,B}] + C.rows × inner_probe cost = 15 + 3033 + 500 × 3 (A inner of AB) = 4548 Est. rows = card(C ⋈ AB)

div.h3: Selected: {A,C} then B or {A,B} then C
div.p: Minimum cost = 4548
